perturbation.original_text: • Each Party shall ensure (i) that all persons mentio ned in Paragraph 1.2.2 above are made aware, prior to any disclosure, of the confidential nature of th e Confidential Information and of the contents of t his Agreement and (ii) [that such persons are bound by confidentially with respect to the Confidential Information as If they were a party to this Agreeme nt.
perturbation.changed_text: • Each Party shall ensure (i) that only the legal counsel is made aware, prior to any disclosure, of the confidential nature of th e Confidential Information and of the contents of t his Agreement and (ii) [that such persons are bound by confidentially with respect to the Confidential Information as If they were a party to this Agreeme nt.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires 'all persons mentioned in Paragraph 1.2.2' (employees, advisors, representatives) to be made aware of the confidential nature of the information. The modified text limits this to 'only the legal counsel'. This creates an in-text contradiction because other clauses still assume that employees, advisors and representatives will handle confidential information and be made aware of its nature, creating an in-text contradiction in the section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: • All Confidential Information received by a Party sh all be held in complete confidence by such Party and by the Party’s directors, employees, advisors o r representatives, and shall not without the prior written consent of the other Party be used for any purpose other than in connection with the purpose for which such Confidential Information was disclos ed or received. • A Party shall not disclose any Confidential Informa tion received by it to any person, unless explicitly instructed to do so by the disclosing party, irrespective of whether the person is an employee, advisor, or representative, and regardless of their concern with the purpose for which such Confidential Information was disclosed o r received or whether they need to know such Confidential Information. • Each Party shall ensure (i) that only the legal counsel is made aware, prior to any disclosure, of the confidential nature of th e Confidential Information and of the contents of t his Agreement and (ii) [that such persons are bound by confidentially with respect to the Confidential Information as If they were a party to this Agreeme nt.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires 'all persons mentioned in Paragraph 1.2.2' to be made aware of confidential information. The changed text limits this awareness to 'only the legal counsel'. The contradicted text states that 'All Confidential Information received by a Party sh all be held in complete confidence by such Party and by the Party’s directors, employees, advisors o r representatives'. Limiting the awareness to only legal counsel directly contradicts the assumption that other parties like employees, advisors, and representatives will handle and be aware of the confidential information as stated in the contradicted text. This creates a strong, direct conflict, making the answer 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall have in effect and maintain adequ ate security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information from unauthorized access, disclosure, u se, and/or misappropriation.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties shall have in effect and maintain minimum security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information from unauthorized access, disclosure, u se, and/or misappropriation.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'adequate' to 'minimum' weakens the security requirements. This introduces ambiguity because what constitutes 'minimum' is subjective and could be interpreted differently, potentially contradicting the intent of protecting Confidential Information. The term 'adequate' is not defined, which is supposed to mean what is adequate in the view of both parties, now is minimum, which is subjective from each party. This section's contradiction, creates problems when the enforcement comes, and create an In-Text Contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: • Each Party shall ensure (i) that only the legal counsel is made aware, prior to any disclosure, of the confidential nature of th e Confidential Information and of the contents of t his Agreement and (ii) [that such persons are bound by confidentially with respect to the Confidential Information as If they were a party to this Agreeme nt. • The Parties shall have in effect and maintain minimum security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information from unauthorized access, disclosure, u se, and/or misappropriation. 1.3 Notwithstanding Sub-clause 1.1, the obligations und ertaken by a Party under this Agreement to maintain confidentiality shall not apply to the extent that the Party is or becomes under an obligation to disc lose Confidential Information (i) by order of a court of competent jurisdiction, or (ii) under statutory la w including according to The Danish Access to Public Administration Files Act, provided that where possi ble the Party shall first notify the other Party of suc h obligation and upon request allow the other Party to advance any defense against such obligation where a ppropriate.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'adequate' to 'minimum' security measures introduces ambiguity, as 'minimum' is subjective. This contradicts the intention of protecting confidential information, as the original text implied a standard that was adequate in the view of both parties. The contradicted text references the need to maintain 'minimum' security measures. The modified text creates an in-text contradiction because the weakened 'minimum' requirement now conflicts with the expectation implied by the original term 'adequate', leading to uncertainty about the level of security required and making enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall be liable in accordance with Danis h law for damages (including claims, expenses and indirect losses of whatsoever nature) arising out o f or in connection with that Party’s breach of its obligations under this Agreement, without prejudice to other rights and remedies available including, but not limited to, restrictive injunction and other eq uitable relief.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party shall be liable in accordance with Danis h law for damages (including claims, expenses and indirect losses of whatsoever nature) arising out o f or in connection with that Party’s breach of its obligations under this Agreement, provided that such damages do not exceed 1000 DKK, without prejudice to other rights and remedies available including, but not limited to, restrictive injunction and other eq uitable relief.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that each party is liable for damages according to Danish law. The modified text adds a maximum liability of 1000 DKK. This creates an in-text contradiction because Danish law might prescribe damages far exceeding 1000 DKK, potentially creating a conflict with the earlier statement regarding liability under Danish law. This contradiction comes in the form of, what will happen if the Danish law says the damage is 10.000 DKK. Will the contract be valid? What is the impact of limiting damage?
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.  Remedies 3.1 Each Party shall be liable in accordance with Danis h law for damages (including claims, expenses and indirect losses of whatsoever nature) arising out o f or in connection with that Party’s breach of its obligations under this Agreement, provided that such damages do not exceed 1000 DKK, without prejudice to other rights and remedies available including, but not limited to, restrictive injunction and other eq uitable relief. 3.2 A Party’s payment of damages or the application of any other remedies shall not release such Party fr om its obligations of confidentiality as set forth in this Agreement. 3.3 The Parties make no warranties, expressly or implie d, in this Agreement with respect to the accuracy a nd completeness of and ownership or rightful access to the Confidential Information, and shall bear no responsibility in this respect.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that each party is liable for damages according to Danish law, implying unlimited liability as prescribed by that law. The modified text introduces a maximum liability of 1000 DKK. The contradicted text is the modified text itself, and this modification directly creates an in-text contradiction. Danish law might prescribe damages far exceeding 1000 DKK, which then directly conflicts with the capped liability of 1000 DKK in the modified text. This limitation creates a clear contradiction and ambiguity regarding the enforceability of the damages clause.
